data.body:
# Jira↔rebar sync: automated bridge, REB cutover, and bidirectional-sync findings

## Context
Configured + shipped automated Jira sync for project REB (navasage.atlassian.net),
then drove the live cutover and a series of correctness fixes. This log preserves
the FINDINGS — especially the still-UNFIXED ones — before we fix them.

## Shipped + merged (PR #20, on main)
- rebar.toml [jira] url/user/project=REB.
- .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml (*/20 cron) + reconcile-bridge-canary.yml
  + docs/jira-sync-setup.md. Repo vars/secret wired (JIRA_URL/USER/PROJECT,
  ACLI_VERSION=1.3.19-stable, ACLI_SHA256, JIRA_API_TOKEN). timeout-minutes raised
  15->60 (one-time bulk sync is serial via acli ~4s/issue; commit-back only on a
  COMPLETED pass). README links the setup doc.
- Reconciler does NOT push its own event writes -> commit-back step pushes when
  the tickets branch is dirty OR ahead of origin.

## Bugs found + FIXED during cutover (merged in PR #20 unless noted)
1. Cross-project targeting (bug 626d / bind-slice-mire): inbound JQL was hardcoded
   `project = DIG` (fetcher.py) — now scoped to jira.project; outbound cross-project
   guard (applier _apply_batch) refuses writes to a project != jira.project.
2. Re-target migration: scripts/retarget_jira_project.py clears DIG bind-state so the
   store re-creates fresh in REB. Validated 1415/1017-DIG -> 398/0-DIG; live cutover
   converged (404/404), 406 REB issues, no duplicates (dedup via rebar-id labels).
3. Over-length description: Jira limits the **ADF representation** (not plain text);
   text_to_adf wraps each line in a paragraph node (~1.5x inflation). adf.fit_text_to
   _adf_limit binary-searches so the ADF JSON fits (<=32000). 46k-char epic now syncs.

## PR #28 (branch fix/assignee-unmappable-converge) — OPEN, assignee + inbound-field sync
- Assignee convergence (bug 9b94): outbound differ resolves the local assignee on the
  would-emit path; unmappable -> desired-unassigned -> converge (no churn).
- Exact-match in validate_assignee_exists (no fuzzy first-result -> no mis-assign,
  e.g. loop-agent !-> "Jira Triage Agent").
- Normalized match: a local assignee that UNIQUELY normalizes (lowercase, alnum-only)
  to one user resolves (joe-oakhart -> Joe Oakhart) while ambiguous (joe -> 3 Joes)
  and coincidental (loop-agent) stay unresolvable.
- INBOUND FIELD SYNC FIX: outbound differ used unconditional local-wins, reverting
  Jira-side edits. Added prev_snapshot-aware gate in _diff_fields: for an inbound-
  mirrored field (title/description/priority/status/assignee), if local == prev_snapshot
  (unchanged since last sync) suppress the outbound so the inbound differ mirrors the
  Jira-side change; genuine local change still local-wins; no prev -> degrades to
  local-wins. Live-verified: assign in Jira -> synced to local (then reverted).
- Tests: 1010 reconciler tests pass; ruff clean. NOTE: PR #28 has grown to cover
  assignee + inbound-field-sync — consider splitting for review.

## OPEN FINDING — INBOUND COMMENT SYNC IS BROKEN (to fix next)
Experiment (REB-5 / local 0241-c6c1-0a20-491b, started 0/0 comments): added 1 LOCAL
comment (rebar comment) + 1 JIRA comment (acli) to the same ticket, then reconciled.
Result: **Jira=2 (works), local=1 (BROKEN)** — the Jira-origin comment was NOT mirrored
to local. RECON showed `inbound_differ total=275 with_comments=0` (zero inbound comment
imports across ALL tickets).

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed by code):
- inbound_differ.compute_inbound_mutations(jira_snapshot, binding_store, local_by_id,
  outbound_mutations) takes NO client.
- inbound_differ._diff_comments_inbound(jira_fields, local_ticket) reads comments from
  `jira_fields["comment"]` — but the live SEARCH snapshot does NOT include comment data
  (Jira search omits comments; documented in outbound_differ). So the inbound differ
  sees 0 Jira comments and imports none.
- The OUTBOUND differ DOES fetch comments (client.get_comments, for dedup) but that
  fetched data is not shared with the inbound differ.

FIX APPROACH (proposed):
- Give the inbound comment path the Jira comments. Options: (a) thread the outbound
  differ's fetched comments into compute_inbound_mutations / the inbound snapshot; or
  (b) pass a client to compute_inbound_mutations so _diff_comments_inbound can fetch
  via get_comments (bounded, like the outbound path); or (c) include comment data in
  the snapshot entries the inbound differ sees (e.g. reuse the absent_alive GET path).
  Outbound comments carry the `<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->` marker so the inbound
  side already filters our own echoes; dedup is by jira_comment_id. RED test: a
  Jira-origin comment (no marker, not in local) must yield an inbound add.

## OTHER NOTED FINDINGS (lower priority / by design)
- Inbound label/comment mirroring is ADD-ONLY: a label/comment REMOVED in Jira is NOT
  removed locally. Likely intentional (convergent add-wins) but worth confirming.
- Many fleet tickets have non-canonical local assignees (joe-oakhart, joe, claude,
  loop-agent, config-loop). With PR #28: joe-oakhart -> Joe (converge keeping Joe);
  joe/agent-names -> one-time unassign -> converge.

## State / hygiene
- Test tickets reverted to clean: REB-6 & REB-291 unassigned (jira+local); REB-5
  comments 0/0. All live test mutations undone.
- Local .tickets-tracker reset to origin/tickets. Worktree:
  .claude/worktrees/jira-sync-automation, branch fix/assignee-unmappable-converge.
- The */20 cron runs on main (PR #20). PR #28 not yet merged -> cron still uses the
  OLD assignee handling until #28 lands.

## NEXT STEPS
1. Fix inbound comment sync (above) — give the inbound differ access to Jira comments.
2. Decide on PR #28 scope (split assignee vs inbound-field-sync?) and merge.
3. (Optional) decide on inbound removal propagation for labels/comments.
